free_text: Evaporation residue excitation function measurements
in 50Ti- and 54Cr- induced reactions with lanthanide
targets
Data for reactions induced by 48Ca shown in figs.5,6
of the reference are compiled in EXFOR entry C2127.
The experiments were carried out at
the Cyclotron Institute at Texas A&M University using
K500 superconducting cyclotron.
Evaporation residua were separated and
momentum analysed using momentum achromat magnetic
recoil spectrometer MARS. Mars was tuned to transmit
most probable charge state of the residue.
The evaporation residues were implanted and
detected at the MARS focal plane by an X1 design
16-strip position-sensitive silicon strip detector
having an active area of 50 x 50mm2.
To distinguish implantation events a large-
area (44-cm2) microchannel plate (MCP) detector was
installed upstream of the Si strip detector.Coincident
signals in the MCP and the Si strip detector indicated
an implantation event, whereas radioactive decay events
were seen only in the PSSD.
The evaporation residua of the present reactions were
identified by their characteristic alpha-decay energies
Beam of 50Ti7+ was accelerated to energy of
4.9MeV/A. Enriched 50Ti sample with enrichment of
0.658+-0.018 was produced from 50TiO2 sample by CaH2
reduction
54Cr beam was prepared from metallic powder enriched
Estimated absolute error of the measured
cross sections
Compiled by S.H.
